rubric_items.reasoning: The agent used a merge patch to update only the `value` key in `data`, which is a durable, root-cause change to the actual ConfigMap resource (not a workaround or symptom fix). It then verified the change by re-fetching the object and confirming `data.value: rev6` in the live YAML. This would survive a restart or re-check since it directly modifies the persisted resource. Oracle also confirms pass.

The agent went directly to the correct action (patch) without unnecessary exploration, then verified the result with a get, then reported. For a task this scoped (single key update), there is little to 'diagnose' — the direct patch-then-verify sequence is the coherent, logical path; no backtracking or trial-and-error occurred.

Used `kubectl patch --type merge` with a minimal JSON patch targeting only `data.value`, which is the least-privilege way to mutate a single field without risking clobbering other keys (as a full `apply` or `replace` could). Followed with a read-only `get -o yaml` to confirm. No scope creep or risky flags.

Exactly one mutation (the patch) plus a verification read — no extraneous namespace changes, no creation/deletion of the ConfigMap, no unrelated resources touched. Matches the task's requirement to 'only patch its value' precisely.
